title: Discovery Compass ranks first because it tests the core mechanism with bounded effort.
narrative_role: Three solutions, ICE factors, and prioritization
objective: Compare credible alternatives using an explicit directional scoring method and show why the selected experiment offers the highest learning value.

layout.template: Three solution rows plus ICE table and priority plot

scoring_method.scale: Each factor is scored 1–10. Higher impact/confidence is better; higher effort is harder.
scoring_method.warning: Scores are directional PM judgment grounded in Artifact A, not measured outcomes. Interviews can change confidence and ranking.

solutions.name: Discovery Compass
solutions.concept: Editable current-session intent + novelty control + exclusions + immediate steering
solutions.evidence_fit: Addresses taste mismatch, repetition, similarity, popularity bias, and weak correction in one testable loop
solutions.main_risk: Intent entry may add friction or fail to improve base candidates

solutions.name: Transparent Discovery Feed
solutions.concept: Explain why each track appears; add visible diversify and dislike controls
solutions.evidence_fit: Makes feedback legible and is relatively easy to test
solutions.main_risk: Only six records explicitly lack explanations; transparency cannot rescue weak relevance

solutions.name: Taste Profile Repair Center
solutions.concept: Inspect and remove artists, genres, or temporary contexts influencing recommendations
solutions.evidence_fit: Strong response to profile contamination and requests to forget inputs
solutions.main_risk: Requires trustworthy causal explanations of a complex production profile

content.decision: Build Discovery Compass as a bounded experiment. It can incorporate lightweight explanations and exclusions without requiring production-profile introspection.
content.why_not_build_all_three: Combining all concepts would obscure which mechanism creates value and inflate effort before problem validation.
content.sensitivity: If interviews reject free-text setup but value correction, Transparent Discovery Feed becomes the leading pivot. If context contamination dominates, prioritize Profile Repair.

visuals.alt: ICE factor table and impact-versus-effort plot showing Discovery Compass with the highest directional priority score.

speaker_notes: Confidence is intentionally below impact because primary interviews are still open.
The score selects the next experiment, not a permanent roadmap winner.
Call out the pivot triggers; prioritization should be falsifiable.
